summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/spl-token with postinstall exfiltration and remote command execution
details: Package name @solana-labs/spl-toke is a one-character omission of the legitimate @solana-labs/spl-token. The package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that fires automatically on npm install. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname is read in multiple locations, lines 10/25/27), POSTs the harvested data to a remote endpoint (line 46), and uses curl to fetch and execute external content (line 103). The combination of name impersonation of a widely-used Solana SPL token package, automatic execution at install time, host fingerprinting, outbound POST of host data, and curl-based remote command execution is an unambiguous installer-targeted supply-chain attack: any developer or build system that mistypes the package name pulls attacker-controlled code onto their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
